title: H3 Extension of DuckDB
text: {duckh3} provides fast, memory-efficient functions analysing manipulating large spatial non-spatial datasets using H3 hierarchical indexing system R. bridges DuckDB’s H3 extension R’s data spatial ecosystems — particular {duckspatial}, {dplyr}, {sf} — can leverage DuckDB’s analytical power without leaving familiar R workflow.

title: H3 Extension of DuckDB
text: {duckh3} built around API duckspatial R package, operates regular R data frames, tibbles, dbplyr lazy tables, duckspatial_df objects. Unlike purely spatial workflows, H3 operations require data spatial. table longitude/latitude columns, existing H3 index column, valid starting point. DuckDB connection used, H3 operations run inside connection H3 extension enabled, letting DuckDB apply query optimisations data reaches R. Results returned lazily materialised explicitly collect . addition, {duckh3} registers set DuckDB macros default connection load time, making H3 functions available directly inside dplyr::mutate() lazy tables — wrapper function needed. Note work lazy tables, regular data frames.

title: H3 Extension of DuckDB
text: functions follow ddbh3_*() prefix (DuckDB H3), structured around expected input data, converted : ddbh3_lonlat_to_*() — longitude/latitude coordinates H3 representations ddbh3_points_to_*() — spatial point geometries H3 representations ddbh3_h3_to_*() — convert H3 cells representations ddbh3_vertex_to_*() — convert H3 vertexes representations following available transformations: also set function retrieve check properties data: ddbh3_get_*() — retrieve H3 cell properties (resolution, parent, children, vertices…) ddbh3_is_*() — check properties H3 indexes (valid, pentagon, Class III…)

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: dbdir String. Either "tempdir", "memory", file path .duckdb .db extension. Defaults "memory". threads Integer. Number threads use. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use available cores detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected number cores might total number cores node, per-job allocation). memory_limit_gb Numeric. Memory limit GB. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use 80% available operating system memory detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected memory might full node memory, per-job allocation). bigint String. handle 64-bit integers. One "integer64" "numeric" ... parameters passed DBI::dbConnect()

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
code: # \donttest{ # load packages library(duckspatial) #> duckspatial 1.0.0 attached #> * Compatible with DuckDB v1.5.1 #> * This release introduces breaking changes #> * See full release notes for migration guidance #>  #> Default output has changed: #>   duckspatial now returns lazy `duckspatial_df` (dbplyr) objects #>   instead of `sf` objects. #>  #> To restore the previous behaviour: #>   ddbs_options(duckspatial.mode = 'sf') library(duckh3)  # create a duckdb database in memory conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "memory", threads = 1)  # create an in-memory connection with 1 thread and 2GB memory limit conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(threads = 1, memory_limit_gb = 2)  # Create a persistent database in disk # conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "my_database.duckdb")  ddbs_stop_conn(conn) # }

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: Convert spatial point geometries H3 cell representations specified resolution. Input must duckspatial_df, sf, table point geometries

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
code: ddbh3_points_to_spatial(   x,   resolution,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_points_to_h3(   x,   resolution,   new_column = "h3string",   h3_format = "string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: x Input data. One : duckspatial_df lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr. sf spatial data frame. tbl_lazy lazy data frame dbplyr. data.frame standard R data frame. character string table view name conn. character vector vector values operate vectorized mode (requires conn = NULL). resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result h3_format Character. Output format H3 cell index. Either "string" (default) "bigint". used ddbh3_points_to_h3().

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: One following, depending inputs: tbl_lazy x spatial. duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df object). TRUE (invisibly) name provided, table created connection TRUE returned invisibly. vector x character vector conn = NULL, function operates vectorized mode, returning vector length x.

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: two functions differ output format: ddbh3_points_to_h3() returns H3 cell index containing point, either string UBIGINT depending h3_format ddbh3_points_to_spatial() returns H3 cell hexagon polygon containing point spatial geometry

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
code: ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(duckspatial)  ## Setup the default connection with h3 and spatial extensions ## This is a mandatory step to use duckh3 functions ddbh3_default_conn(threads = 1)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Convert to duckspatial_df points_ddbs <- ddbs_as_points(points_tbl)  ## TO H3 strings/ubigint ------------  ## Add column with h3 strings at resolution 8 points_strings_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_h3(points_ddbs, 8)  ## Add column with h3 ubigint at resolution 10 points_bigint_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_h3(   points_ddbs,    resolution = 8,   new_column = "h3bigint",   h3_format = "bigint" )  ## TO SPATIAL -----------------  ## Convert from POINTS to H3 POLYGONS of res 8 polygons_8_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_spatial(points_ddbs, 8)  ## Collect as sf polygons_8_sf <- ddbs_collect(polygons_8_ddbs)

text: x Input data. One : duckspatial_df lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr. sf spatial data frame. tbl_lazy lazy data frame dbplyr. data.frame standard R data frame. character string table view name conn. character vector vector values operate vectorized mode (requires conn = NULL). n Integer. Vertex number retrieve. Must range 0–5 hexagons 0–4 pentagons. used ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(). h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) new_column Name new column create input data. title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: One following, depending inputs: tbl_lazy x spatial. duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df object). TRUE (invisibly) name provided, table created connection TRUE returned invisibly. vector x character vector conn = NULL, function operates vectorized mode, returning vector length x.

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: functions cover full vertex workflow: ddbh3_h3_to_vertex() returns single vertex H3 cell, identified vertex number n (0–5 hexagons, 0–4 pentagons), H3 vertex string ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes() returns vertices H3 cell H3 vertex strings — either nested (one row per cell) unnested (one row per vertex) depending nested ddbh3_vertex_to_lat() returns latitude H3 vertex string ddbh3_vertex_to_lon() returns longitude H3 vertex string ddbh3_vertex_to_spatial() converts H3 vertex strings spatial point geometries. input column nested, vertices automatically unnested aggregated MULTIPOINT geometry per cell
